claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child Alex. Can you help me figure out if I'm eligible?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: how old is Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 19 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know Alex is 19. Since Alex is over 16, I need to ask a few more questions to determine eligibility.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? This would include things like:
- A-levels or equivalent
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to Level 3
- A course at a further education college

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education. He's doing A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that Alex is studying A-levels. That counts as approved education.

Just to clarify a few more details:

Is Alex's A-level course considered full-time (more than 12 hours a week of supervised study or course-related work)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's a full-time course - more than 12 hours a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now, when did Alex turn 19? (Or alternatively, when is Alex's birthday?)

I ask because Child Benefit for a child in approved education usually stops on 31 August after they turn 19, unless they started their current course before turning 19.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex turned 19 in February this year, so he was already 18 when he started the A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful.

One more question: does Alex live with you, or do they live elsewhere?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a couple more questions to confirm eligibility.

1. Are you responsible for Alex? This means you pay for things like food, clothes, and accommodation for him.

2. Do you (and Alex) live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex - I pay for his food, clothes, and everything he needs. And yes, we both live in the UK, in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me just check the specific rules regarding Child Benefit eligibility to make sure I give you accurate information about Alex's situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: The guidance mentions that Child Benefit continues if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education. Let me check the more detailed guidance about what happens when a child turns 16 to understand the specific age rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the rules I've reviewed, I need to share some important information with you:

**Key rule:** You can get Child Benefit until your child turns 20 if they're in approved education or training, **but only if they were accepted onto the course before they turned 19**.

You mentioned that Alex turned 19 in February this year and was already 18 when he started his A-levels. This is a crucial detail. Alex would need to have been accepted onto his current A-level course **before** he turned 19 to remain eligible.

Could you confirm: Did Alex start his A-levels before he turned 19 in February? (For example, did he start in September of the previous year when he was still 18?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he started the A-levels in September last year when he was still 18, so before he turned 19 in February.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's very helpful information.

Just one final question to complete the assessment: Does Alex receive Universal Credit in his own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't get Universal Credit or any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise what you've told me:

- Alex is 19 years old (turned 19 in February this year)
- He is studying A-levels full-time (more than 12 hours per week)
- He started the A-level course in September last year, before he turned 19
- He lives with you in Manchester, UK
- You are responsible for him (paying for food, clothes, etc.)
- Alex does not receive Universal Credit or any other benefits in his own name

Based on the eligibility rules, I'm now ready to provide my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

✅ **Age:** Although Alex is 19, Child Benefit can continue until he turns 20 as long as he remains in approved education.

✅ **Approved education:** A-levels count as approved non-advanced education.

✅ **Full-time study:** Alex is studying more than 12 hours per week.

✅ **Started before turning 19:** Crucially, Alex started his A-level course in September when he was still 18, which meets the requirement that he must have been accepted onto the course before turning 19.

✅ **No disqualifying benefits:** Alex does not receive Universal Credit in his own name.

✅ **Responsibility and residence:** You are responsible for Alex and you both live in the UK.

**Important things to note:**
- You must tell HMRC that Alex is staying in education or training if you haven't already.
- Child Benefit will stop on 31 August after Alex turns 20, or when he completes/leaves his A-level course, whichever comes first.
- If Alex's circumstances change (e.g., he leaves education, starts working 24+ hours per week, or starts receiving certain benefits), you'll need to inform HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) He is under 20 years old (currently 19). (2) He is in approved full-time education (A-levels, which count as non-advanced education, studying more than 12 hours per week). (3) He was accepted onto and started his A-level course before he turned 19 (started in September when he was 18, turned 19 in February). (4) He does not receive Universal Credit or other disqualifying benefits in his own name. (5) He lives with the claimant in the UK. (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex, paying for his food, clothes, and other needs. All conditions for continued Child Benefit eligibility for a child aged 16-19 in approved education are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex is 19, he remains eligible because he is in full-time approved education (A-levels), started the course before turning 19, does not receive Universal Credit, and lives with the claimant who is responsible for him in the UK. Child Benefit can continue until Alex turns 20 or completes/leaves his course, whichever comes first.

(2) He is in approved full-time education (A-levels, which count as non-advanced education, studying more than 12 hours per week). (3) He was accepted onto and started his A-level course before he turned 19 (started in September when he was 18, turned 19 in February). (4) He does not receive Universal Credit or other disqualifying benefits in his own name. (5) He lives with the claimant in the UK. (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex, paying for his food, clothes, and other needs. All conditions for continued Child Benefit eligibility for a child aged 16-19 in approved education are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex is 19, he remains eligible because he is in full-time approved education (A-levels), started the course before turning 19, does not receive Universal Credit, and lives with the claimant who is responsible for him in the UK. Child Benefit can continue until Alex turns 20 or completes/leaves his course, whichever comes first.
